title: Red Flags in AI Data Responses Every Manager Should Know
summary: Some AI answer patterns signal trouble before anyone checks the file.

content.summary: Some AI answer patterns signal trouble before anyone checks the file.

body.text: Fluent wrong answers look more dangerous than obvious errors because they bypass skepticism. Round numbers without breakdowns, trends with no date column cited, or rankings that omit your known top customer should trigger immediate chart checks. Answers that ignore your active filters—discussing all regions when you filtered to EMEA—mean the model lost subset context.

body.alt: Professional reviewing business charts on a laptop
body.caption: Professional reviewing business charts on a laptop. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Round numbers without breakdowns, trends with no date column cited, or rankings that omit your known top customer should trigger immediate chart checks.
Answers that ignore your active filters—discussing all regions when you filtered to EMEA—mean the model lost subset context.

items: Suspiciously round totals on messy real data.
No reference to columns you know exist.
Narrative contradicts a chart you built five minutes ago.

body.text: Stop the slide deck. Re-apply saved rules, rebuild the chart, re-ask with explicit column names.
Log red-flag incidents to improve export quality and prompt templates.
Managers using Datilio can spot red flags early because preview and filters sit next to Talk to Data.

meta_description: Some AI answer patterns signal trouble before anyone checks the file.
meta_keywords: Red Flags, AI Quality, Management, Risk
